results.rendered_html:
abhi-√car (Ved. Inf. abhi-carita-vai,  TBr. , and abhi-caritos,  Kāṭh. ; cf.  Pāṇ. iii, 4, 13 , Sch.)
to act wrongly towards any one;
to be faithless (as a wife);
to charm, enchant, bewitch,  RV. x, 34, 14  (Subj. 2. pl. -caratāBi/),  AV.  &c.;
pūrvāBicaritA (f. perf. Pass. p.) = pūrva-dig-gā-minī,  R. i, 34, 10.

results.rendered_html:
abhicar 1 P.
1 To act wrongly towards any one, offend, trespass; tatra virādhadanukabaṃdhaprabhṛtayaḥ kepyabhicaraṃtīti śrutaṃ  Mv. 2. 
2 To be faithless (as wife or husband); patiṃ yā nābhicarati manovāgdehasaṃyatā  Ms. 5. 165 ; 9. 102. 
3 To charm, conjure, exorcise (by spells or incantations), employ spells for magical purposes &c.; vṛṣṭyāyuḥ puṣṭikāmo vā tathaivābhicarannapi  Y. 1. 295 ; 3. 289. 
4 To possess, occupy.
